title: Computing Goodwill in an Acquisition
event: Hollis Co. pays $900,000 to acquire Delgado Co. The fair value of Delgado's identifiable net assets (assets minus liabilities) is $720,000. Compute goodwill and state its subsequent treatment.

computationPaths.narration: Goodwill is the residual — what the buyer paid beyond every asset it could name and value.

steps.label: Subsequent treatment
steps.formulaText: NOT amortized — tested annually for impairment

memorize.body: Goodwill = purchase price − fair value of identifiable net assets acquired.

Goodwill is recorded ONLY when purchased in an acquisition — internally generated goodwill is never booked. And a bargain purchase (price < FV of net assets) produces a GAIN, not negative goodwill on the balance sheet.

questions.prompt: Purchase price $900,000, FV of identifiable net assets $720,000. Goodwill = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the full purchase price. Goodwill is only the residual ABOVE the identifiable net assets: 180,000.
That's the identifiable net assets — the part that ISN'T goodwill. The unexplained premium is 180,000.
